sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Washoe County, "Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream" listing
sources.quotedText: Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream. July 15 through September 30. Daily: 0 trout, catch and release only. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

sources.title: USGS 10336715 — MARLETTE CK NR CARSON CITY, NV (streamgage on Marlette Creek, the outlet stream of Marlette Lake, elev. ~7,760 ft near the lake outlet)
sources.quotedText: MARLETTE CK NR CARSON CITY, NV

sources.title: Marlette Creek SR 28 Crossing and Realignment Project — Nevada Tahoe Conservation District
sources.quotedText: This project near Thunderbird lodge on Lake Tahoe's east shore proposes to restore a portion of Marlette Creek to improve stream function, water quality, and fish habitat and provide erosion control and stormwater treatment at directly connected outfalls from SR 28.

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 860476, "Marlette Creek", Stream, Carson City, NV
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":860476,"gaz_name":"Marlette Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Carson City (city)"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13350970.6466, 4745321.5855) converts to lon -119.93381, lat 39.16491 (mouth, near Lake Tahoe's east shore), ~427 m from the file's recorded coordinate (-119.92998, 39.16735), confirming it within tolerance. The second multipoint vertex (-119.90640, 39.17251) sits near the Marlette Lake outlet (the stream's headwater).

parameters.note: Marlette Creek is the natural outlet stream of Marlette Lake, flowing west down the Carson Range to Lake Tahoe's east shore near Thunderbird Lodge (USGS gage 10336715 'Marlette Ck nr Carson City' sits high near the lake outlet at ~7,760 ft; USGS Marlette Creek sampling points sit at the Lake Tahoe interface at ~6,227 ft = lake level). Because it is that outlet stream, it is governed by CR 25-16's Washoe County listing 'Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream', which opens the whole Marlette Lake system to fishing only July 15 through September 30 (catch-and-release), and closes it the remainder of the year. Marlette Lake is an NDOW Lahontan-cutthroat/rainbow broodstock lake, which is why the system is catch-and-release. The representative reach is in Washoe County; the lowest reach at the Lake Tahoe mouth lies within the Consolidated Municipality of Carson City, but the water is a single named outlet stream and the Washoe 'Marlette Lake … outlet stream' listing controls it.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe County — Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream)
regulations.humanSummary: Open to fishing only July 15 through September 30, catch-and-release only; closed the rest of the year (Marlette Lake outlet-stream special regulation).
regulations.verbatimText: Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream. July 15 through September 30.

parameters.counting_rule: 0 trout may be kept — catch-and-release only. Applies to all trout in the connected Marlette Lake system (the lake holds Lahontan cutthroat, rainbow, and brook trout per NDOW), which descend into the outlet stream.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe County — Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream)
regulations.humanSummary: Catch-and-release only — 0 trout may be kept, daily or in possession.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 0 trout, catch and release only.

parameters.note: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Washoe County — Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Marlette Creek is a Nevada water in the Carson Range, not the open water of Lake Tahoe, so the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal-license arrangement does not apply here — a Nevada license is required. Access is via the Marlette Lake trail system out of Spooner Lake (Lake Tahoe Nevada State Park); confirm trail/park status before a trip.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
